rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SANDRA MARGARITA ESPINOZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/2023 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 10/19/2023). The following additional/updated notes were e-mailed to counsel on 10/17/2023: 1) The second amendment must be signed by both petitioners. At least one petitioner must verify. 2) No supplemental inventory and appraisal has been filed for additional property received. 3) A legible copy of the Schwab account statement covering 05/31/2023 must be filed with a declaration re retention of the original statement required by LRSF 14.59(A). 4) Total charges must match total credits. 5) The calculation of statutory compensation is incorrect. Please also clarify whether the petitioners intended to add income during account period ($2,628.31) + additional property received ($2,301.02) to the fee base. 6) No creditor claim has been filed by Francisco Espinosa for funeral expenses. See section 9000(a)(3). 7) Josefina Cupido's power of attorney did not specifically grant a power to disclaim her interest in an estate. See section 4264(d). 8) The notary acknowledgment attached to Josefina Cupido's power of attorney is defective, as it failed to comply with Civil Code section 1189(a)(3) (notary must sign "under penalty of perjury"). 9) An allegation must be made as to how the reserve balance, if any, should be distributed, together with any unknown or later-discovered property (if an omnibus clause is desired). The proposed order must be revised to incorporate the allegation. Each beneficiary and assignee's share must be specified. An appearance may be required on 11/22/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the outstanding issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT ALAN BLACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Amended petition has been filed, which raises additional issues: a. Proof of Publication has not been filed on the filing of the amended petition (an amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends (including publication) [California Rules of Court 7.53(a)]). b. Notice of Petition to Administer the Estate was sent and filed before the amended petition was filed. c. Caption of the petition is incomplete. d. Value of estate is not specific (states is $70-$120k). e. Item 5a is incomplete. f. Item 6 is incorrect, which must mark the first box that applies. g. Item 7 is incomplete. h. Waiver of Bond attached to the petition is incomplete (name of the personal representative at Item 4). Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT BRADLEY STRAHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/23 (the earliest available date). The following issues need to be supplemented. (1) Allowance or Rejection of Creditor's Claim [DE-174] to be filed for each Creditor's Claim. Petition paragraph 14 merely lists claims on file; however, the personal rep shall allow or reject the claim in whole or in a part regardless of the solvency of the estate. PC sec. 9250. (2) If the claims are rejected, the petition cannot be heard until the 90-day passes from the rejection. (3) Caption to set forth petition seeks approval of the personal rep's Creditor's Claim. CRC 7.10250 and LR 14.29. (4) Then, Notice of hearing to describe the complete caption once it is revised. CRC 7.50. (5) Consent to this petition from the creditors whose claims have not been satisfied. (6) The omnibus clause to set forth priority of unpaid claims/expenses when the funds become available. PC sec.9251. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MATILDA CYTRON TRUST U/D/T DATED MARCH 20, 1978, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition 1 For Order Determining Questions Of Interpretation Of Trust Probate Code 17200b1 And Instructing Trustee To Follow Terms Of Trust 2 For Ord
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/06/2023. The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 2) The proofs of service attached to the DE-120 forms did not list Leonard Horowitz. 3) The petition caption and item 1 of the DE-120 forms did not refer to an order directing Leonard to distribute Lenora's share as additional relief sought. CRC 7.50 & 7.102. 4) Paragraph 8 of the petition: i) The addresses of all beneficiaries and trustees (other than the petitioner and Sally Gati) must be stated. Probate Code section 17201 & CRC 7.902. ii) Daniel Cytron is not listed. iii) David Gati is not listed. iv) Is Chris Lenz a beneficiary? If so, his address must be stated. 5) Lenora's ownership interest in the Benedict Canyon Property at the time of her death is alleged to be 48.74%, but according to Exhibit C (attorney's letter to Sally Gati), the percentage is 42.685%. 6) Please specify the percentage of Shirley's ownership interest in the Benedict Canyon Property at the time of her death. 7) Is the petitioner's claim for breach of trust timely under section 16460(a)(2)?
